mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 60-day return or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and output growth, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to generate positive returns during recovery periods, or INDPRO growth correlates negatively with equity returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 58% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal predictive power vanishes when real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of occurrences or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which increases corporate revenue baselines and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 52% of high-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high interest rates signals demand destruction from monetary tightening, leading to immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when INDPRO declines during high-rate environments, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward validation.
